title: Unity Software Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript Key Takeaways
text: Financial Performance: Unity exceeded guidance significantly in Q4 2024. Revenue from the strategic portfolio reached $442 million, up 4% year-over-year, while adjusted EBITDA of $106 million beat guidance by 26%, delivering 23% margins. Create Solutions grew 9% YoY with subscription revenue climbing 15%. The company noted that announced price increases haven't yet impacted results, rolling out through 2025-2026. Grow Solutions posted 2% YoY growth, marking the best quarter in a year, driven by strong seasonal demand and improved execution. Strategic Initiatives: Unity Vector Migration: The company announced moving its ad network to Unity Vector, an AI-powered platform launching end of Q1 2025 with initial migration complete by Q2. CEO Matt Bromberg cautioned this represents iterative work requiring patience as the system scales. Create Business Momentum: Nearly 38% of active users upgraded to Unity 6, exceeding expectations. The engine maintains dominance with 70% of top 1,000 mobile games built on Unity. Industry Segment Success: The fastest-growing subscription business surged 50% YoY, with major wins including Toyota and Raytheon partnerships. Forward Guidance: Q1 2025 expectations: Revenue of $405-$415 million; adjusted EBITDA of $60-$65 million. Management attributed lower guidance partly to Vector transition disruption.
